summary: The United States Defense Advanced Research Projects Agency’s Quantum Benchmarking Initiative is expanding, inviting new organizations and approaches to evaluate whether any quantum computing architecture can achieve utility-scale—where computational value exceeds cost—by 2033. Since mid-2024, the initiative has assessed 20 companies’ qubit architectures, with eleven advancing to deeper development and two reaching system-level validation. Micah Stoutimore succeeds Joe Altepeter as managing director, stating that “it now seems likely that someone will build a utility-scale quantum computer by 2033, but it remains unclear exactly which team or teams might get across that finish line.” Abstracts for new applicants are due July 31, 2026, with full proposals by September 30, 2026. This program aims to add rigor and realism to quantum computing evaluation.
